sections.paragraph:
rarely defect. גָּדֹל Gen 1:16 constr. גְּדֹול, גְּדֹל,three times in גְּדָל־ קרי Psal. 145:8 Nah 1:3 Pro 19:19.

(1) great, of magnitude and extent, הַיָּם הַגָּדֹול Num 34:6 [“הָאָדָם הַגָּדֹול בָּעֲנָקִים a large (tall) man among the Anakim, Jos 14:15 ”]; of number and multitude, as גֹּוי גָּדֹול Gen 12:2 of violence, as of joy, Neh 8:12 of mourning, Gen 50:10 of importance, Gen 39:9 Joe 2:11 Gen 29:7, עֹוד הַיֹּום גָּדֹול “as yet the day is great,” i.e. there is yet much day left. French, grand jour; Germ. hoch am Tage; LXX. ἔτι ἐστὶν ἡμέρα πολλή. Subst. גְּדֹל זְרֹועֲךָ magnitude of thy arm; Exo 15:16. Plur. גְּדֹלֹות great actions, things done nobly, especially of God, Job 5:9 9:10 37:5 .

Specially-(a) elder, eldest. Gen 10:21, אֲחִי יֶפֶת הַגָּדֹול “the eldest brother of Japhet” [this should be, “the brother of Japhet the eldest;” see the accents]; Gen 27:1, בְּנֹו הַגָּדֹל “his eldest son;” Gen 15:12.-(b) great of power, nobility, wealth, powerful. Exo 11:3 2 K. 5:1 Job 1:3. הַכֹּהַן הַגָּדֹול “the great (i.e. the high) priest,” Hag 1:1, 12 Hag 1:12, 14 Hag 1:14. Pl. גְּדֹלִים nobles, Pro 18:16 גְּדֹלֵי הָעִיר 2Ki 10:6, 11 2Ki 10:11.

(2) proud, compare Hithpael, No. 2. Psa 12:4, לְשֹׁון מְדַבֶּרֶת גְּדֹלֹות “a tongue speaking proud things,” i.e. magniloquent, impious things (compare Dan 7:8, 11 , 20 11:36 Rev 13:5, and Gr. μέγα εἰπεῖν, Od. xvi. 243, xxii. 288).
